area_anchor: Brikette — Guest booking lookup is repeated unnecessarily during email sync and recovery
current_truth: The guest email map (which maps sender email addresses to booking records) is rebuilt from the database on every sync batch and on every individual thread recovery attempt. For large booking databases, this redundant work adds latency to sync and recovery operations.
next_scope_now: Build the guest email map once per sync or recovery batch and reuse it across all threads in that batch, rather than rebuilding per thread.
adjacent_later: TTL-based guest map cache that persists across sync batches
evidence_refs: operator-stated: guest email map rebuilt on every sync/recovery batch instead of cached across threads
why: Every time the system processes a batch of emails or retries failed drafts, it queries the booking database to build a lookup of which email addresses belong to which guests. It does this separately for each thread instead of once for the whole batch. This wastes database queries and slows down email processing. Caching the lookup for the duration of a batch means faster sync and fewer unnecessary database calls.

intended_outcome.statement: Guest email map is built once per sync/recovery batch and reused across all threads in that batch, eliminating redundant database queries.
